content:
I'm getting this warning. Are we missing any flags?

rust-binder.o: warning: objtool: _RNvMs_NtCshGTzrjO2YYN_5alloc3vecINtB4_3VecINtNtCs1KjjdLpqqp9_11rust_binder11range_alloc5RangeNtNtBJ_10allocation14AllocationInfoEE6removeBJ_() falls through to next function __cfi__RNvMso_NtCs2T9Dodn8vEz_6kernel6rbtreeINtB5_14RawVacantEntrylINtNtNtB7_4sync3arc3ArcNtNtCs1KjjdLpqqp9_11rust_binder6thread6ThreadEE6insertB1n_()

Not sure what you mean by flags.

Typically that warning is objtool not knowing about a function being noreturn at the end of the symbol, so it thinks it falls through.

Normally, it is just a matter of adding the right symbol to the list (or a pattern) so that the tool can understand the control flow.
Yeah, so if you have e.g. a panic around there, it may be just that we don't have the annotation for one of those yet (which sometimes it changes with the Rust version too, since core changes).
I recently sent https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250112143951.751139-1-ojeda@kernel.org/ -- it may be that you need that one.

Miguel Ojeda said:

I recently sent https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250112143951.751139-1-ojeda@kernel.org/ -- it may be that you need that one.

I will send that one as a fix this rc or the next, even if it is not really a fix, since 1.85 will soon be released.
